last_item: web/Version — the two irreversible typed-confirm gates in web/src/views/Version.tsx: FinalizeDialog (permanently deletes the old cluster, 'point of no return', no rollback after) and RollbackDialog (permanent data loss — discards every write to the new major since the upgrade). Both are the panel's most destructive one-click actions and had NO test (no Version.test.tsx existed), so the confirm-on-risky invariant here was entirely unproven (band-1 correctness)
last_result: SHIPPED (test only, no bug): added web/src/views/Version.test.tsx (2 tests) driving the real path via the exported PendingFinalizationBanner. Pins the load-bearing detail that each dialog demands a DIFFERENT number — finalize wants the OLD major (from_major, the cluster it deletes), rollback wants the NEW major (to_major, the version it abandons). With from=16/to=17 each test types the OTHER dialog's number first (must stay DISABLED), a superstring '169'/'179' (must stay DISABLED), a numeric-equivalent '16.0'/'17.0' (must stay DISABLED), then its own value whitespace-padded ' 16 '/' 17 ' (must ENABLE), then asserts the destructive API fires only after the gate and with the correct major; each also asserts the irreversible-consequence copy is on screen before the operator can act. No bug — both gates correct; tests lock the contract. Mutation-proven over 13 one-line mutations (perl apply + git checkout revert, tree clean each time): finalize from->to cross-wire, rollback to->from cross-wire, drop each disabled={busy||!matches} gate, drop .trim(), cross-wire each API arg, and loosen ===expected to .includes/.startsWith/Number() on each dialog. test-skeptic caught that the first draft (only wrong-exact + right-exact inputs) let a substring/prefix/numeric-coercion gate stay green — a fat-fingered '169'/'16.0' would fire the irreversible delete; added the superstring + numeric-equivalent rejects before commit and re-verified all escapes now red. code-reviewer clean (faithful real-path test, mocks api/toast only, matches RolesDatabases.test.tsx conventions). Web gate green: typecheck clean, build OK, 167/167 vitest. Backend gate untouched (no Go changed); gofmt clean at start. e2e not applicable (pure RTL).
notes: Never-ending hardening loop. Bias: (A) prove vibe-coded code actually does what it claims with tests that fail if behavior breaks; (P) preflight every risky/destructive/external op and fail loud+actionable BEFORE acting; (D) tighten defaults + self-heal so a bad config can't wedge Postgres. Re-seed the backlog via a parallel audit panel (Mode S) when it runs thin — this loop does NOT self-complete. Shared defaults live in scripts/ralph/DEFAULTS.md; real sm behavior at /primary01/git/server-management/src/sm/. Run go/make outside the sandbox if snap-confine blocks it.
north_star: An indie hacker runs a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI on best defaults — never loses data, never gets wedged, never confused. Every risky path is preflighted, every failure is loud and actionable, every claim is covered by a test that would fail if the behavior broke.
